heading: Maritime Security Advisory Committees
label: 70112 Maritime Security Advisory Committees
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (b)(4)(B). , substituted “.” for “section 10(c) of the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.).”Pub. L. 117–286, § 4(a)(294)section 1009(c) of title 5Subsec. (b)(7). , substituted “Chapter 10 of title 5” for “FACA” in heading and “Chapter 10 of title 5” for “The Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.)” in text.Pub. L. 117–286, § 4(a)(295)2018—, amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section required Secretary to establish a National Maritime Security Advisory Committee and authorized Secretary to establish an Area Maritime Security Advisory Committee for any port area of the United States.Pub. L. 115–282, § 602(a)Subsec. (a)(1)(A). inserted “, including on enhancing the sharing of information related to cybersecurity risks that may cause a transportation security incident, between relevant Federal agencies and—” in introductory provisions and added cls. (i) to (vi).Pub. L. 115–2542010—Subsec. (b)(5). , amended par. (5) generally. Prior to amendment par. (5) read as follows: “The membership of an Area Maritime Security Advisory Committee shall include representatives of the port industry, terminal operators, port labor organizations, and other users of the port areas.”Pub. L. 111–281, § 810(1)Subsec. (g)(1)(A). , substituted “2020;” for “2008;”.Pub. L. 111–281, § 810(2)(A)Subsec. (g)(2). , substituted “2018” for “2006”.Pub. L. 111–281, § 810(2)(B)2006—Subsec. (b)(5). realigned margins.Pub. L. 109–2412004—Subsec. (b)(5). added par. (5).Pub. L. 108–293

Treatment of Existing Committee, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–282, title VI, § 602(b)132 Stat. 4293Dec. 4, 2018“Notwithstanding any other provision of law—“(1)an advisory committee substantially similar to the National Maritime Security Advisory Committee established under , United States Code, as amended by this section, and that was in force or in effect on the day before the date of enactment of this section [], may remain in force or in effect for a period of 2 years from the date of enactment of this section, including that the charter, membership, and other aspects of the committee may remain in force or in effect; andsection 70112(a) of title 46Dec. 4, 2018“(2)during the 2-year period referenced in paragraph (1)—“(A)requirements relating to the National Maritime Security Advisory Committee established under , United States Code, as amended by this section, shall be treated as satisfied by the substantially similar advisory committee; andsection 70112(a) of title 46“(B)the enactment of this section, including the amendments made in this section, shall not be the basis—“(i)to deem, find, or declare such committee, including the charter, membership, and other aspects thereof, void, not in force, or not in effect;“(ii)to suspend the activities of such committee; or“(iii)to bar the members of such committee from meeting.”

Cybersecurity Information Sharing and Coordination in Ports, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–254, div. J, § 1805(a)132 Stat. 3533Oct. 5, 2018“(a).—Maritime Cybersecurity Risk Assessment ModelThe Secretary of Homeland Security, through the Commandant of the Coast Guard and the Under Secretary responsible for overseeing the critical infrastructure protection, cybersecurity, and other related programs of the Department of Homeland Security, shall—“(1)not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act [], coordinate with the National Maritime Security Advisory Committee, the Area Maritime Security Advisory Committees, and other maritime stakeholders, as necessary, to develop and implement a maritime cybersecurity risk assessment model, consistent with the activities described in section 2(e) of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act (), to evaluate current and future cybersecurity risks that have the potential to affect the marine transportation system or that would cause a transportation security incident (as defined in , United States Code) in ports; andOct. 5, 201815 U.S.C. 272(e)section 70101 of title 46“(2)not less than biennially thereafter, evaluate the effectiveness of the cybersecurity risk assessment model established under paragraph (1).”

Information Sharing, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–254, div. J, § 1805(c)(2)132 Stat. 3534Oct. 5, 2018“The Commandant of the Coast Guard and the Under Secretary responsible for overseeing the critical infrastructure protection, cybersecurity, and other related programs of the Department of Homeland Security shall—“(A)ensure there is a process for each Area Maritime Security Advisory Committee established under , United States Code—section 70112 of title 46“(i)to facilitate the sharing of information related to cybersecurity risks that may cause transportation security incidents;“(ii)to timely report transportation security incidents to the national level; and“(iii)to disseminate such reports across the entire maritime transportation system via the National Cybersecurity and Communications Integration Center; and“(B)issue voluntary guidance for the management of such cybersecurity risks in each Area Maritime Transportation Security Plan and facility security plan required under , United States Code, approved after the date that the cybersecurity risk assessment model is developed under subsection (a) of this section [set out as a note above].”section 70103 of title 46

children.heading: National Maritime Security Advisory Committee.—
children.label: a National Maritime Security Advisory Committee.—

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: 1 .—Establishment
children.text: There is established a National Maritime Security Advisory Committee (in this subsection referred to as the “Committee”).

children.label: A
children.text: State, local, and tribal governments;

children.label: B
children.text: relevant public safety and emergency response agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: relevant law enforcement and security organizations;

children.label: D
children.text: maritime industry;

children.label: E
children.text: port owners and operators; and

children.label: F
children.text: terminal owners and operators.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Committee shall consist of at least 8 members, but not more than 21 members, appointed by the Secretary in accordance with this subsection and .section 15109 of this title

children.heading: .—Expertise
children.label: B .—Expertise
children.text: Each member of the Committee shall have particular expertise, knowledge, and experience in matters relating to the function of the Committee.

children.label: i
children.text: Port authorities.

children.label: ii
children.text: Facilities owners and operators.

children.label: iii
children.text: Terminal owners and operators.

children.label: iv
children.text: Vessel owners and operators.

children.label: v
children.text: Maritime labor organizations.

children.label: vi
children.text: The academic community.

children.label: vii
children.text: State and local governments.

children.label: viii
children.text: The maritime industry.

children.heading: .—Distribution
children.label: D .—Distribution
children.text: If the Committee consists of at least 8 members who, together, satisfy the minimum representation requirements of subparagraph (C), the Secretary shall, based on the needs of the Coast Guard, determine the number of additional members of the Committee who represent each entity specified in that subparagraph. Neither this subparagraph nor any other provision of law shall be construed to require an equal distribution of members representing each entity specified in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: .—Administration
children.label: 4 .—Administration
children.text: For purposes of , the Committee shall be treated as a committee established under chapter 151 of such title.section 15109 of this title

children.heading: Area Maritime Security Advisory Committees.—
children.label: b Area Maritime Security Advisory Committees.—
i
children.text: establish an Area Maritime Security Advisory Committee for any port area of the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: request such a committee to review the proposed Area Maritime Transportation Security Plan developed under section 70103(b) and make recommendations to the Secretary that the committee considers appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: may advise, consult with, report to, and make recommendations to the Secretary on matters relating to maritime security in that area;

children.label: ii
children.text: may make available to the Congress recommendations that the committee makes to the Secretary; and

children.label: I
children.text: the Secretary, who shall call such a meeting at least once during each calendar year; or

children.label: II
children.text: a majority of the committee.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Each committee established under this subsection shall consist of at least 7 members appointed by the Secretary, each of whom has at least 5 years practical experience in maritime security operations.

children.heading: .—Terms
children.label: B .—Terms
children.text: The term of each member of a committee established under this subsection shall be for a period of not more than 5 years, specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Notice
children.label: C .—Notice
children.text: Before appointing an individual to a position on a committee established under this subsection, the Secretary shall publish a notice in the Federal Register soliciting nominations for membership on the committee.

children.heading: .—Background examinations
children.label: D .—Background examinations
children.text: The Secretary may require an individual to have passed an appropriate security background examination before appointment to a committee established under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Representation
children.label: E .—Representation
children.text: Each committee established under this subsection shall be composed of individuals who represent the interests of the port industry, terminal operators, port labor organizations, and other users of the port areas.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Each committee established under this subsection shall elect 1 of the committee’s members as the Chairperson and 1 of the committee’s members as the Vice Chairperson.

children.heading: .—Vice chairperson acting as chairperson
children.label: B .—Vice chairperson acting as chairperson
children.text: The Vice Chairperson shall act as Chairperson in the absence or incapacity of the Chairperson, or in the event of a vacancy in the office of the Chairperson.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, and the head of any other interested Federal agency may, designate a representative to participate as an observer with a committee established under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Role
children.label: B .—Role
children.text: The Secretary’s designated representative to a committee established under this subsection shall act as the executive secretary of the committee and shall perform the duties set forth in .section 1009(c) of title 5

children.heading: .—Consideration of views
children.label: 5 .—Consideration of views
children.text: The Secretary shall consider the information, advice, and recommendations of each committee established under this subsection in formulating policy regarding matters affecting maritime security.

children.label: i
children.text: compensation at a rate fixed by the Secretary, not exceeding the daily equivalent of the current rate of basic pay in effect for GS–15 of the General Schedule under including travel time; andsection 5332 of title 5

children.label: ii
children.text: travel or transportation expenses under .section 5703 of title 5

children.heading: .—Status
children.label: B .—Status
children.text: A member of a committee established under this subsection shall not be considered to be an officer or employee of the United States for any purpose based on the receipt of any payment under this paragraph.

children.heading: 105.—Chapterof title
children.label: 7 105.—Chapterof title
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 does not apply to a committee established under this subsection.
